matches.moment: Yamal's individual carry through the right half-space breaks the deadlock — a genuine individual-quality gap, not a coin-flip game.
Lukaku's second-half introduction shifts momentum late — a substitute-impact game-state swing reflecting the scrappier, tighter profile of this tie.

verdict: Today's slate splits cleanly between a genuine class gap and a genuine coin flip. Spain's numbers are the standout of the round of 16: zero goals conceded in four tournament matches, a 0.275 xGA per 90 that leads the field, and a clean-sheet record built partly against comparable opposition rather than pure group-stage padding — that defense should be enough to see off a Portugal side that has been blanked once already this tournament and leans heavily on Ronaldo's individual moments rather than a settled attacking process. The USA-Belgium tie is the opposite story: two defenses that have both shown real cracks against the best opposition they've faced so far, a market that is nearly dead-even, and two teams whose paths to the round of 16 both required a late scare rather than comfort. Treating that match as a probable favorite win would be the model imposing certainty the data doesn't support; the draw is the honest call. If both results hold, the emerging quarterfinal picture rewards defensive solidity over raw attacking volume — Spain's clean-sheet discipline looks like the most transferable asset left in the bracket. — The Analyst.

players.name: Lamine Yamal

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
